title: Triage and fix 5 moderate npm audit findings
description: During TASK-004 setup the Orchestrator ran `npm install` and npm surfaced 5 moderate severity vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies of devDeps (vitest, ajv, ajv-formats, gray-matter). They were intentionally deferred so they didn't bloat the TASK-004 implementation diff. Triage now: run `npm audit`, classify each finding (false positive vs. real path-to-exploit in our test runtime), apply `npm audit fix` where safe, document any breaking changes that would require devDeps bumps, and rerun `npm test` to confirm no regression. Not deploy-relevant — this is a tooling-only repo — but worth resolving before more devDeps land.
acceptance_criteria: `npm audit` reports 0 moderate-or-higher findings (or each remaining one is documented as accepted with an explicit rationale in a NOTES section in this ticket).
`npm test` runs and the test pass/fail count matches the count immediately before the audit fix (no regressions introduced).
Any devDep version bumps required by the fix are documented in the commit body with the version delta and a one-line reason.
package.json and package-lock.json are both committed in the same commit so they stay in sync.
